name: Problem statement clarity
description: The research problem is clearly stated, specific, and well-scoped. The reader understands exactly what is being investigated and why it matters.

name: Research questions defined
description: Specific research questions or hypotheses are explicitly stated. Questions are measurable and directly tied to the problem statement.

name: Literature review coverage
description: Relevant prior work is reviewed and cited. The review demonstrates awareness of the current state of the field and identifies gaps.

name: Gap identification
description: The submission clearly identifies what is missing or unresolved in existing literature and explains how this work addresses that gap.

name: Methodology described
description: The research methodology is described in sufficient detail. Another researcher could replicate the approach from the description provided.

name: Research design justification
description: The choice of research design is justified. The authors explain why this method is appropriate for the research questions.

name: Evaluation methodology
description: Evaluation approach is clearly defined including metrics, baseline comparisons, and statistical significance testing where appropriate.

name: Dataset description
description: Datasets used are described including source, size, splits, and any preprocessing steps applied. Limitations of the dataset are acknowledged.

name: Baseline comparison
description: Results are compared against at least one meaningful baseline. The baseline is described and justified as an appropriate point of comparison.

name: Results reported clearly
description: Results are presented with sufficient detail including numerical values, confidence intervals or error bars, and clearly labeled tables or figures.

name: Statistical significance
description: Claims of improvement are supported by statistical significance tests. P-values or equivalent measures are reported where applicable.

name: Limitations acknowledged
description: The authors explicitly discuss limitations of the study including scope, dataset, methodology, and generalizability of findings.

name: Ethical considerations
description: Ethical implications of the research are addressed including data privacy, potential misuse, and bias in datasets or models.

name: Contribution clearly stated
description: The specific contributions of the paper are explicitly listed. The authors clearly state what is novel about this work.

name: Related work differentiated
description: The submission explains how this work differs from the most closely related prior work. Similarities and differences are made explicit.

name: Results interpreted
description: Results are not just reported but interpreted. The authors explain what the results mean in the context of the research questions.

name: Future work identified
description: The submission identifies concrete directions for future research that follow naturally from the findings and limitations.

name: Abstract completeness
description: The abstract covers the problem, approach, key results, and conclusion. A reader can understand the full contribution from the abstract alone.

name: Writing clarity
description: The paper is written clearly and precisely. Technical terms are defined. Arguments flow logically from section to section.

name: References formatted correctly
description: All cited works are included in the reference list. References follow a consistent format. No broken or missing citations.
